original_query: select min(i_item_id),         min(ca_country),         min(ca_state),         min(ca_county),         min(cs_quantity),         min(cs_list_price),         min(cs_coupon_amt),         min(cs_sales_price),         min(cs_net_profit),         min(c_birth_year),         min(cd_dep_count)  from catalog_sales, customer_demographics, customer, customer_address, date_dim, item  where cs_sold_date_sk = d_date_sk and        cs_item_sk = i_item_sk and        cs_bill_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk and        cs_bill_customer_sk = c_customer_sk and        cd_gender = 'F' and        cd_education_status = 'Secondary' and        c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk and        d_year = 2001 and        c_birth_month = 1 and        ca_state in ('MS', 'NY', 'VA')        and cs_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 65 AND 70        AND i_category = 'Women' ;
rewritten_query:
select min(i_item_id),         
       min(ca_country),         
       min(ca_state),         
       min(ca_county),         
       min(cs_quantity),         
       min(cs_list_price),         
       min(cs_coupon_amt),         
       min(cs_sales_price),         
       min(cs_net_profit),         
       min(c_birth_year),         
       min(cd_dep_count)  
from catalog_sales
JOIN date_dim ON cs_sold_date_sk = d_date_sk AND d_year = 2001
JOIN item ON cs_item_sk = i_item_sk AND i_category = 'Women'
JOIN customer_demographics ON cs_bill_cdemo_sk = cd_demo_sk AND cd_gender = 'F' AND cd_education_status = 'Secondary'
JOIN customer ON cs_bill_customer_sk = c_customer_sk AND c_birth_month = 1
JOIN customer_address ON c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk AND ca_state IN ('MS', 'NY', 'VA')
WHERE cs_wholesale_cost BETWEEN 65 AND 70;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Rewrite the query using explicit JOIN syntax instead of comma-separated tables in the FROM clause with WHERE conditions. This makes the join order explicit and moves filtering conditions directly to their respective JOIN clauses.
